free_text: Interaction of fast neutrons with Ho-165
last author.
2.8 cm diameter, 2.55 cm high sample of holmium
metal weighing 143.03 g and located 15 cm
from the target.
TOF used in conjunction with attenuation
method for total cross-section measurements.
NE213 liquid scintillator coupled to a
philips XP1040 photomultiplier tube for
the scattering experiments.
From NEUDADA, some originally from private
communication, Zago, 28/01/1972, some from
text of publication.
Reaction string in Subent 002
and data columns in Subent 003 corrected.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents.
Differential elastic scattering with a contribution
from low lying inelastic levels.
Normalization by comparison
with scattering from a polyethylene sample.
Angular distributions corrected for multiple
scattering with the Monte-Carlo code (see REL-REF).
Statistical errors
Total error mainly due to uncertainty
of the efficiency curve of the neutron detector.
From NEUDADA, originally given in private
communication, Zago, 28/01/1972.
SD: SF3=EL -> SCT; SF5=PAR in REACTION
code. (Reaction sum replaced by one code).
DATA-ERR -> ERR-T. E-LVL-MAX added. STATUS updated.
Obtained from scattering
measurements on a polythene sample.
Errors range from 5 to 15% and are
mainly due to uncertainty in the width of the
energy intervals.
A further error of 10% arises because of the
uncertainty in the detector efficiency as a function
of energy.
From NEUDADA, originally given in private
communication, Zago, 28/01/1972.
SD: ERR-ANALYS, STATUS updated.
Minimum exit neutron energy
Maximum exit neutron energy
Nuclear temperature obtained from statistical plot
of inelastic scattering data at 6 and 7.5 MeV,
taking the mean of the values at each energy.
Data from the text of Nucl.Phys.,A133(1969)572
Errors given are fitting uncertainties only.
